licence.note: Community project; verify the licence on whichever fork you install.

securityPolicy.note: Community project — no formal disclosure policy at this time. Open issues for any concerns.

signedReleases.note: npm provenance attestations on the upstream fork; check before installing.

codeReview.note: Single-maintainer / small-team project. Public PR history. Smaller review surface than corp-maintained MCPs.

oauthHandling.note: Figma personal access tokens are account-wide. No per-team scoping available.

filesystemAccess.note: Image exports may write to a configured directory; scope at the host.

dataResidency.note: Figma's infrastructure (US, EU options for Enterprise tier).

defaultPermissions.note: PAT inherits user permissions across all Teams. For larger orgs, use a dedicated bot user.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Layer names, component descriptions, comments can carry user-authored text. Lower exposure than browser MCPs because the structured-data shape constrains what tooling sees.

supplyChainSurface.note: MCP SDK + Figma's official sdk + a few utility deps. Verify the fork you install — community MCPs vary in dep hygiene.

rateLimitRisk.note: Figma API rate limits per token. Large design-file reads can saturate.

maintainerType.note: Active community project; small maintainer team.

verdictScope: Design-to-code workflows that read Figma as the source of truth.
verdictNote: Use it for design-to-code. Read-only by design — that's the right call. For larger orgs, use a dedicated bot Figma user with project-scoped membership rather than a personal PAT. Verify the specific fork you install before deploying.
